skill.name: infinite-gratitude
skill.description: Coordinate parallel research across multiple AI agents, with guidance that points users to the upstream Infinite Gratitude project.
skill.summary: Runs structured multi-agent research workflows for broad topics.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found hardcoded GitHub URLs and a shell command example that clones an external repository. These are transparent source references without user-controlled command construction, while the weak-cryptography alerts are false positives from prose lines rather than cryptographic code.

low_findings.title: External Repository Clone Instruction
low_findings.description: The skill includes a hardcoded git clone example for an external GitHub repository. The command is not built from user input, so command injection was not found, but users are directed to fetch code outside the packaged skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell command is visible and hardcoded, with no interpolation or user-controlled input. The remaining risk is dependency on external repository contents that are not included in this package.

low_findings.title: External GitHub References
low_findings.description: The skill points to an upstream GitHub repository in metadata, body text, and installation guidance. No evidence found of hidden network requests or data exfiltration, but the skill depends on external documentation and source code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All network indicators are explicit GitHub URLs. They are consistent with source attribution and install guidance rather than concealed outbound communication.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography alerts occur on descriptive prose and section text, not cryptographic APIs. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, encryption, hashing, or credential handling in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain natural-language description content. There is no cryptographic implementation or algorithm selection in the package.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded Shell Command Example
dangerous_patterns.description: A bash block shows a git clone command for the upstream repository. The command is fixed and does not create an injection path, but it asks users to retrieve external code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The fenced bash block and git clone command are directly present. Risk is limited because the command has no dynamic arguments.

content.value_statement: Large research tasks can stall when one agent follows a single path. This skill frames research as a multi-agent workflow, so Claude, Codex, or Claude Code can divide topics and synthesize findings.
actual_capabilities: Describes a parallel research workflow that uses multiple agents.
Explains when to use the skill for broad or extensive research topics.
Directs users to the upstream Infinite Gratitude repository for full setup details.
Provides a direct clone command for the upstream repository.
Identifies the upstream project source in both metadata and user-facing text.
limitations: The package contains only SKILL.md and no executable orchestration code.
Full installation and usage details are delegated to an external repository.
The packaged file does not define agent roles, prompts, or synthesis templates.
Research quality depends on the connected AI tool, sources, and user review.

use_cases.title: Map a New Research Area
use_cases.description: Break a broad topic into parallel research tracks, then combine the main findings into a single overview.

use_cases.title: Compare Product Opportunities
use_cases.description: Assign agents to market, competitor, customer, and technical questions before summarizing the strongest options.

use_cases.title: Investigate Technical Decisions
use_cases.description: Use parallel agents to examine implementation options, risks, evidence, and tradeoffs for a complex decision.

prompt_templates.title: Research a Topic
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a broad overview.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use infinite-gratitude to research this topic with parallel agents: [topic]. Return the main themes, strongest evidence, and open questions.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Several Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Use infinite-gratitude to compare these options: [options]. Assign separate agents to benefits, risks, costs, adoption barriers, and evidence quality.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Evidence Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use infinite-gratitude to create a parallel research plan for [question]. Define agent workstreams, source types, synthesis criteria, and validation checks.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate a Deep Investigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use infinite-gratitude for a deep investigation of [problem]. Split work across specialized agents, resolve conflicts between findings, and produce an executive synthesis.

output_examples.input: Research the current state of privacy-preserving analytics for a product team.
output_examples.output: A workstream plan covering market context, technical approaches, vendor landscape, compliance issues, and adoption risks, followed by a concise synthesis.

output_examples.input: Compare three infrastructure choices for a new data platform.
output_examples.output: A side-by-side decision brief with evidence quality notes, risk ratings, unresolved questions, and a recommended next research step.

output_examples.input: Investigate whether a startup should enter a specific customer segment.
output_examples.output: A research summary that separates customer demand, competitive pressure, pricing assumptions, distribution constraints, and validation gaps.

best_practices: Define the decision or research question before starting the parallel workflow.
Ask agents to cite source quality and uncertainty, not only conclusions.
Review synthesized findings manually before using them for business or technical decisions.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill for simple questions that one concise answer can resolve.
Do not treat parallel agent agreement as proof without checking source quality.
Do not clone or run external repository code without reviewing it first.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps organize broad research tasks into parallel agent workstreams and synthesis steps.

faq.question: Does the package include runnable orchestration code?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed package contains SKILL.md and points to an upstream repository for setup.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks the skill as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this skill best for short questions?
faq.answer: No. It is more useful for broad topics that benefit from parallel investigation.

faq.question: What should I verify before using the upstream repository?
faq.answer: Review the repository contents, installation steps, dependencies, and any commands before running them.

faq.question: Does this skill replace human research review?
faq.answer: No. Users should verify sources, assumptions, and conclusions before relying on the output.
